projectDescription: Sherlock is an AI-powered research and investigation workspace that helps users run structured analyses, manage findings, and explore entity relationships across customizable domain scopes.
keyFeatures: AI-powered investigation runs across multiple domains (government fraud, cybersecurity, geopolitics, competitive intelligence, and more)
Visual research boards built on tldraw for canvas-based investigation and entity mapping
Workspace-grounded chat with streaming AI responses and @mention references
Timeline view for chronological research history with saved snapshot views
Network graph for entity relationship visualization with D3.js
Live Monitor for real-time signal scanning and alert generation
Files browser for managing workspaces, artifacts, and canonical workspace items
Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) support for Gemini, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Anthropic
Browser-local SQLite storage — no server database required, all data stays in your browser
Full workspace export and import in HTML, Markdown, and JSON formats
publicApiSurface: Run Setup modal (scope, domain pack, purpose, model, date range, priority sources)
Workspace CRUD (create, rename, icon, delete, purge)
Artifact viewer (section editing, evidence, key findings, follow-ups, export)
Research Board (multi-board canvas, library placement, board-agent sessions)
Chat (workspace-grounded sessions, @mentions, transcript export, guided run mode)
Timeline (chronology filters, saved views, snapshot export)
Network Graph (manual nodes/links, entity resolution, flag/hide, board handoff)
Live Monitor (scan configuration, source filters, auto-save to signals)
Files (workspace browser, artifact/item grid and list, deep-link focus)
Settings -> Data (backup export, import, clear workspace data)
Settings -> Scopes (custom scope creation and management)
Settings -> Themes (theme workbench, accent colors, dark/light mode)
